Container.Name: auto-mcs
Container.Overview:
auto-mcs is a cross-platform Minecraft server manager that creates and manages servers (Vanilla, Paper, Fabric, Forge, and more) in under a minute, with Modrinth mod/plugin browsing, automatic backups, and a scripting console.
This container exposes the auto-mcs interface as a web terminal (ttyd) on the Web UI port. Manage everything from your browser — no desktop client needed.
SECURITY: This template requires you to set a Web UI Password at install (the upstream default is root / auto-mcs — don't use it). The web terminal grants shell access inside the container, so keep it on your LAN unless you've secured it.
PORTS: 8080 is the web UI, 7001 is the Telepath remote-management API, and 25565 is the default port for the Minecraft server auto-mcs runs. auto-mcs opens a new port for every server you create — if you plan to run more than one, set Network Type to "host" so you don't have to add a port mapping for each.
TIP: If Crafty Controller or another Minecraft server already uses port 25565, change the host port here to something free (e.g. 30000).
Container.Changes: ### 2026-06-21 - Initial template for auto-mcs. - Self-hosted icon, no-new-privileges hardening. - Web UI (ttyd), Telepath API, and default Minecraft port mapped. - Require a Web UI password at install (no shipped default) and mask it. - Allocate a TTY (-t -i) to match upstream's ttyd/tmux setup. - Add a TCP healthcheck on the web port so Unraid shows container health. - Drop the redundant WEB_PORT variable; change only the host side of the Web UI Port mapping.

Config.Name: App Data
Config.Description: auto-mcs configuration, all managed servers, world files, and backups.

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: Browser interface (ttyd web terminal). Change only the host (left) side if 8080 is already in use; leave the container side at 8080.

Config.Name: Minecraft Server Port
Config.Description: Default port for the Minecraft server auto-mcs runs. Change the host port if Crafty or another server already uses 25565. Add more port mappings for additional servers.

Config.Name: Telepath API Port
Config.Description: Optional Telepath remote-management API. Only expose this if you use Telepath; keep it off your WAN.

Config.Name: WEB_USERNAME
Config.Description: Username for the web UI login. Default: root.

Config.Name: WEB_PASSWORD
Config.Description: Password for the web UI login. Set a strong password here (the upstream default is 'auto-mcs' — do not use it). The web terminal is a root shell inside the container.

Config.Name: TZ
Config.Description: Timezone for log timestamps, e.g. America/New_York. Defaults to UTC.
